perturbation.original_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Sensitive Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Sensitive Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Sensitive Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Sensitive Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Sensitive Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses the term 'Confidential Information' which is already defined, and refers to what the receiving party will have to adhere to. I have changed this to 'Sensitive Information' which causes an in-text contradiction since the NDA refers to 'Confidential' and now 'Sensitive Information' without defining 'Sensitive', raising questions as to whether these categories are equivalent or distinct. The specific contradiction arises because the agreement dictates handling of 'Confidential Information' and introducing 'Sensitive Information' creates ambiguity about the scope of those obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is identified as proprietary by the Disclosing Party with an appropriate legend or if orally disclosed, then it must be followed up in writing immediately. Disclosing Party - the party from time to time disclosing Confidential Information. 1 Receiving Party - the party from time to time receiving Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently uses 'Confidential Information,' which Clause 1 defines. The changed text replaces this with 'Sensitive Information' without defining 'Sensitive Information.' This introduces ambiguity and a potential contradiction. Clause 1 defines Confidential Information. The changed text now refers to Sensitive Information, creating uncertainty about whether the obligations in Clause 2 apply to information not defined as 'Confidential Information' under Clause 1. This directly conflicts with Clause 1, because Clause 1 provides a specific definition that no longer aligns with all the terms referenced in the NDA.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 4 The Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. The Disclosing Party makes no representation, warranty, assurance, or inducement, express or implied, as to the Confidential Information's adequacy, sufficiency, or freedom from defect of any kind, including but not limited to, freedom from patent infringement that may result from use of such know-how information, nor shall the Disclosing Party incur any responsibility or obligation by reason of such Confidential Information, except as specifically provided herein. The Receiving Party shall under no circumstances obtain any right in the Disclosing Party's patents, trademark or know-how by reason of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 4 The Classified Material shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. The Disclosing Party makes no representation, warranty, assurance, or inducement, express or implied, as to the Classified Material's adequacy, sufficiency, or freedom from defect of any kind, including but not limited to, freedom from patent infringement that may result from use of such know-how information, nor shall the Disclosing Party incur any responsibility or obligation by reason of such Classified Material, except as specifically provided herein. The Receiving Party shall under no circumstances obtain any right in the Disclosing Party's patents, trademark or know-how by reason of the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' in Clause 4. I have replaced this with 'Classified Material.' This creates a contradiction because the document never defines 'Classified Material' and it's unclear if this is a subset, superset, or completely different type of information than 'Confidential Information', introducing ambiguity. The contradiction occurs because the Receiving Party's obligations are tied to 'Confidential Information' yet the ownership now rests on 'Classified Material'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Sensitive Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Sensitive Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Sensitive Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Sensitive Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Sensitive Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information' in Clause 4, while the changed text replaces it with 'Classified Material.' The explanation states that the document never defines 'Classified Material,' making it unclear if it is the same, a subset, or a superset of 'Confidential Information.' Clause 2 (the contradicted text) obligates the Receiving Party to treat 'Sensitive Information' as confidential. The change in Clause 4 from 'Confidential Information' to the undefined 'Classified Material' creates ambiguity regarding which information is covered under the NDA, leading to an in-text contradiction. The change impacts the Receiving Party's obligations as their obligations are tied to 'Confidential Information' but ownership now rests on 'Classified Material'.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, upon 30 days written request by the other Party, return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Confidential Information in computerized form has been destroyed. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall immediately cease to use the Confidential Information for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, upon 15 days written request by the other Party, return to the other party all Protected Data which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Protected Data in computerized form has been destroyed. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall immediately cease to use the Protected Data for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says the Parties have 30 days to return 'Confidential Information'. I've changed this to 15 days and changed 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data.' This creates a contradiction because the NDA never defines 'Protected Data'. This contradicts with the Receiving Party's obligation to return 'Confidential Information' if the NDA specifies that Protected Data will be returned instead, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is identified as proprietary by the Disclosing Party with an appropriate legend or if orally disclosed, then it must be followed up in writing immediately. Disclosing Party - the party from time to time disclosing Confidential Information. 1 Receiving Party - the party from time to time receiving Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is defined in Clause 1. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data,' which is not defined in the NDA. Since Clause 1 defines 'Confidential Information' and not 'Protected Data,' there is a contradiction because the Receiving Party's obligation to return 'Confidential Information' is now replaced with an undefined term. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, which fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 8 The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately if it becomes aware that Confidential Information has been disclosed to or is in the possession of any person who is not authorized by the NDA to receive Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 8 The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately if it becomes aware that Business Secrets has been disclosed to or is in the possession of any person who is not authorized by the NDA to receive Business Secrets.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Receiving Party to notify if 'Confidential Information' is disclosed to unauthorized persons. I've changed this to 'Business Secrets'. Because 'Business Secrets' is not defined anywhere in the NDA, and also Confidential information is already defined. This creates a contradiction regarding what types of disclosures trigger a notification requirement, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Sensitive Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Sensitive Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Sensitive Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Sensitive Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Sensitive Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Sensitive Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Confidential Information,' which is likely a defined term within the NDA (although not provided in the excerpt). The changed text replaces this with 'Business Secrets,' which, as the explanation states, is not defined. Clause 2 in the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Sensitive Information,' mandating specific treatment. By changing 'Confidential Information' to an undefined 'Business Secrets,' and leaving 'Sensitive Information' as the term used elsewhere, a strong ambiguity arises. It becomes unclear whether the notification requirement in Clause 8 applies only to 'Business Secrets,' or if it also includes 'Sensitive Information' as defined elsewhere. This directly contradicts the intention to treat all information in Clause 2 as confidential and triggers the Misaligned Terminology definition.
